description_long: This object is a rectangular outdoor tennis court with a green playing surface and clearly defined white boundary lines. The court is flanked by two long, narrow brown areas that represent the out-of-bounds or run-off zones, and a central net divides the court into two equal halves. The net is supported by two posts at either end, and the court markings include baselines, sidelines, service boxes, and a center service line. There is no visible fencing or enclosure around the court, and no lighting fixtures are present. The overall design is utilitarian, focusing on function and clarity for play.
description: A green rectangular tennis court with white lines, brown run-off zones, and a central net supported by posts. No fencing or lighting fixtures are visible.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the court, central net, and both brown run-off zones.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the court's length, net profile, and the thinness of the surface.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the net and run-off zones visible from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the court's length and the net's side profile.
attribute_net_type: Braided nylon net, black, supported by two metal posts at either end, standard tensioning.
attribute_court_line_markings: All standard tennis lines present, white, medium width, complete and clearly visible.
